Keeping self.mage fallback, capitalized field aliases, Force->forces, and scalar Willpower support is confusing and masks schema/model problems. title: Add profile config-backed auth login stub
description:
Add a Rust-side `auth login` command that does not perform OAuth yet. It resolves a handle/DID through Jacquard, writes a durable profile config under XDG config, and makes that profile the default actor for read commands.

## Direction

- Canonical command: `tassle auth login <did-or-handle>`.
- This is profile login, not token auth yet.
- It must not destroy existing profiles.
- Store profiles as separate TOML snippets under `config.toml.d/`, one file per profile primary key / DID.
- Use the active/default profile for commands whose `--repo`/`--actor` is omitted.
- This is the foundation for later OAuth login and multi-profile support.

## ADR content to capture

- Why profile config exists before OAuth.
- Why config is a directory of TOML fragments instead of a single mutable config file.
- Filename/key strategy for DIDs.
- How active/default profile is represented without destroying others.
- Future figments-rs `.d` expansion integration.
notes: Implemented profile-login stub: `tassle auth login <did-or-handle>` resolves DID/PDS via Jacquard, writes `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-~/.config}/tassle/config.toml.d/<did>.toml`, preserves existing unrelated TOML content, and lets read commands default `--repo`/`--actor` from the newest active profile. ADR written at `doc/adr/0001-profile-config-before-auth.md`.
close_reason: Done: auth login profile stub, default actor config, and ADR implemented.

title: Add Rust mage stats command using actor.rpg.stats/mage
description:
Add a Rust clap command group `tassle mage` with initial subcommand `stats` that reads Mage: The Ascension sheet data from `actor.rpg.stats` using Jacquard XRPC.

## Context

Live record inventory for `jauntywk.bsky.social` / `did:plc:zjbq26wybii5ojoypkso2mso` found both shapes:

- `actor.rpg.stats/mage` exists and uses the per-system envelope shape: `{ system: "mage", data: { ... } }`.
- `actor.rpg.stats/self` also exists and has legacy inline blocks: `{ mage: { ... }, reverie: { ... }, ... }`.
- The `mage` data in both records was byte-for-byte identical at the time of inspection.
- Existing real Mage fields are legacy/capitalized: `Arete`, `Willpower`, `Prime`, `Force` singular, etc.; `quintessence` and `paradox` were absent.

## Implementation direction

- Command shape: `tassle mage stats`.
- Flags should include at least:
  - `--actor <did-or-handle>` / `-a` for target actor; default to dev reference or active account once auth exists.
  - `--rkey <rkey>` for explicit override/debugging.
  - `--json` for raw/normalized JSON.
- Default lookup order:
  1. `actor.rpg.stats/mage` and normalize `{ system, data }`.
  2. fall back to `actor.rpg.stats/self` and normalize inline `mage`.
- Normalize legacy field spelling/case for display: `Force` -> `forces`, Capitalized sphere names -> lowercase keys.
- Show actual source URI/rkey/shape in the output so users can see whether they are reading canonical `/mage` or fallback `/self`.

## XRPC sketch

CLI reads map directly to ATProto repo queries for now:

- Resolve actor handle -> DID/PDS using Jacquard identity resolver.
- GET `com.atproto.repo.getRecord`:
  - `repo`: target DID
  - `collection`: `actor.rpg.stats`
  - `rkey`: `mage`
- On `RecordNotFound`, GET same collection with `rkey`: `self`.

Future server-side equivalent could become an appview query:

- `com.superbfowle.tass.getMageStats`
  - query params: `actor`, optional `campaign`, optional `rkey`, optional `includeRaw`
  - output: normalized mage stats plus `source` metadata (`uri`, `cid`, `shape`, `updatedAt`)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `cargo run -p tassle-cli -- mage stats --actor jauntywk.bsky.social` fetches the live `actor.rpg.stats/mage` record through Jacquard XRPC.
- [ ] Output includes Arete, Willpower, and all nine spheres from the live record, including handling `Force` singular.
- [ ] Missing `quintessence`/`paradox` is displayed as absent rather than zero.
- [ ] `--json` prints normalized data and source metadata.
- [ ] Fallback to `self.mage` works when `/mage` is missing.
notes:
Correction: the intended command is `tassle mage stats`; initial create command lost the backticked text because zsh evaluated it in the ticket title. Also prefer Jacquard public client/session APIs for reads: use `jacquard::client::BasicClient` / `XrpcClient::send(ListRecords)` / `AgentSessionExt::get_record` where available. Avoid a direct `reqwest` dependency in tassle-cli unless Jacquard lacks the needed operation. Direct `tokio` runtime dependency is still expected for async CLI execution.
Implemented first Rust slice: `tassle mage stats` and alias `tassle mage list` read `actor.rpg.stats/mage` first, normalize the per-system envelope, and fall back to `self.mage` if needed. Output treats `actor.rpg.stats` as the player state/history anchor and reports missing quintessence/paradox as absent.
close_reason: Done: mage stats/list implemented and verified against live actor.rpg.stats/mage.

title: Make the Rust Jacquard CLI the primary product path
description:
Rebase tassle's active CLI direction onto the Rust workspace (`crates/tassle-cli`) using clap v4 derive and Jacquard as the atproto/XRPC toolkit.

## Context

The repo already has a Rust workspace with generated Jacquard lexicon types and a small clap CLI skeleton. The previous direction treated Rust as schema/codegen support and TypeScript as product path; that is no longer desired. The Rust CLI should become the main path.

Relevant current files:

- `crates/tassle-cli/src/main.rs` — clap derive CLI skeleton
- `crates/tassle-cli/src/commands/generate/node.rs` — existing generated Node record command
- `crates/tassle-lexicons/` — Jacquard-generated `com.superbfowle.tass.*` types
- `doc/jacquard-use.md` — older note that must be revised because it says not to migrate the TS CLI

## Design direction

- Use clap v4 derive for all command structure.
- Use Jacquard for atproto primitives and XRPC; avoid reimplementing transport/auth.
- For HTTP, align with Jacquard's stack: `reqwest 0.12` via `jacquard-common::http_client::HttpClient` and `XrpcExt`, plus `tokio` runtime.
- Prefer Jacquard bootstrap XRPC request types for `com.atproto.repo.{listRecords,getRecord,createRecord,putRecord,deleteRecord}`.
- Keep generated `tassle-lexicons` types as canonical record builders/validators.
- Use `jiff` for new project-owned time code where possible, but Jacquard currently exposes `chrono`-based `Datetime`; do not fight generated types unnecessarily.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `crates/tassle-cli` has the dependencies needed for async XRPC: `jacquard`, `reqwest`, `tokio` or equivalent through Jacquard.
- [ ] `tassle` Rust binary can perform at least one public XRPC read through Jacquard/reqwest, not raw curl.
- [ ] README/development docs identify the Rust CLI as primary and TS CLI as legacy/reference or remove stale TS-primary wording.
- [ ] `doc/jacquard-use.md` is updated to remove the old "Do NOT migrate the TS CLI to Rust" recommendation.
- [ ] Existing Rust `generate node`/`samples` functionality still builds.
notes: Transport direction refinement: do not add direct `reqwest` usage/dependency just because XRPC is HTTP. Jacquard already wraps `reqwest` behind `jacquard-common::http_client::HttpClient` and exposes public unauthenticated clients plus XRPC traits. First implementation should try `jacquard::client::BasicClient` and bootstrap request types in `jacquard_common::xrpc::atproto` for list/get/create/put/delete. Add direct `reqwest` only if there is a concrete gap.
close_reason: Done: Rust CLI is primary in docs, tassle-cli uses clap + tokio + Jacquard, and repo list performs live public listRecords through Jacquard without direct reqwest usage.

title: tassle-config: add service (non-login) config for the axum oauth web flow
description: Today tassle-config is login-centric: a single Profile{did,handle,pds,auth_mode,session_id,store_path}. The imminent axum OAuth web service needs config that is NOT keyed to a login profile: server bind addr/host, public base URL, OAuth client metadata (client_id/redirect URIs/scopes), turso store location + sync mode, and secret sourcing. Design a service/app config layer in tassle-config that coexists with the login Profile model (don't bolt service knobs onto Profile). Should be consumable by both the CLI and the web service. Relates to the turso auth-store (tass-auth-store-turso) being in good shape to serve oauth sessions, and to tass-app-crate (Runtime::from_profile -> shared tassle-app), whose description is now stale re: 'jac-store-fjall store' (it's turso). Prework — flesh out the design before the web flow lands.
design: Open questions to settle: (1) one figment with [service] + [profiles.*] tables, or a separate ServiceConfig source? (2) where do oauth client secrets live (env/secret store vs config)? (3) turso local-file vs embedded-replica/remote for the server. (4) how the CLI vs server select their config root.

title: tassle-cli: use tassle-config's figment module, delete duplicate config.rs
description: tassle-config/src/config.rs is a near-identical copy of tassle-cli/src/config.rs (same 8 figment fns: tassle_config_dir/config_file/dropins_dir/build_figment/profile_from_env/active_figment/active_profile/active_name/available_profiles); only diff is the profile struct name (LoginProfile vs Profile, the latter adds is_resolvable()). tassle-config's own lib.rs docs already flag this as the planned follow-up. Make tassle-cli depend on tassle-config (non-optional base), replace all crate::config::* with tassle_config::config::*, map LoginProfile->Profile, delete crates/tassle-cli/src/config.rs and its 'mod config;'. profile_config.rs (legacy did-keyed model + toml_edit write helpers) is NOT a duplicate and stays for now.
acceptance_criteria: crates/tassle-cli/src/config.rs deleted; no crate::config:: refs remain; cargo build -p tassle-cli (with and without --features auth-store) passes; behavior of config/auth/list commands unchanged.
close_reason: tassle-cli now depends on tassle-config (non-optional base) and uses tassle_config::config::*; duplicate crates/tassle-cli/src/config.rs deleted; auth-store feature delegates to tassle-config/auth-store. Builds green with and without auth-store; tassle-config tests pass. profile_config.rs (legacy did-keyed + toml writers) intentionally retained.

title: tass-phase: FSM-backed async phased Job + Executor substrate (port zim executor/job)
description: Port the zim-claude executor/job idea into a generic Rust crate built on rust-fsm. Pure FSM machine (phases) + async Driver (effects between transitions, awaits real events, no polling) + concurrent Executor streaming completions. Substrate for tass-engine's action chain and tass-job's durable due-job queue. FSM State derives Serialize so a parked chain can persist/resume (dueAt). tokio async.
design: tass-phase: generic in-memory substrate (Phases + Driver + run + FsmJob + Executor) on rust-fsm. Event-driven (no polling). tass-job and tass-engine build ON this: tass-job = tass-phase + fjall persistence(parked phase via resume/phase) + dueAt sleep_until scheduling + retry/backoff/idempotency; tass-engine action chain = a Phases machine. Executor is single-task FuturesUnordered, non-Send, so jobs can borrow the lent &session.

title: Switch auth-store backend from fjall to turso (jac-store-fjall)
description:
jac-store-fjall gained engine-v2 (domain AuthRepository boundary) + a native-SQL turso backend (TursoRepository implementing RepoCore+OAuthRepo+AppPasswordRepo). Move tassle's app-password auth store off fjall (FjallAuth::open + .app_password()) onto turso for SQL flexibility. Scope: auth store only (tassle-config + tassle-cli); tassle-ledger stays on raw fjall. Decision: turso-only (drop backend-fjall from the auth store), no runtime backend selection.

Call sites: tassle-config/src/auth.rs (Store type alias + for_profile construction), tassle-cli/src/commands/auth.rs (session_status, login_real), tassle-cli/examples/auth_spike.rs. Wiring: flip workspace jac-store-fjall dep to default-features=false, features=[backend-turso]. Turso open is async (open_local(path).await) and uses a single .db file (not a fjall dir), so store_path default changes from {name}.fjall to {name}.db and create_dir_all must target the parent, not the path.
acceptance_criteria: Store alias = AppPasswordStore<TursoRepository>; all 4 call sites build under --features auth-store; cargo build -p tassle-config -p tassle-cli --features auth-store passes; no FjallAuth/FjallEngine/Cbor refs remain in the auth store path; tassle-ledger untouched.
close_reason: Done: auth store on turso (TursoRepository), all 4 call sites + workspace dep switched, builds green under auth-store. tassle-ledger untouched.

title: tass-listend standalone service binary
description: Standalone daemon wiring sources + engine + job worker + config + wide-event tracing + StoreProvider. The primary deployment shape (not CLI-first). Loads two-level config, opens the shared fjall store, runs source tasks and the worker under tokio::select, graceful shutdown.

title: Constellation catch-up + idempotent dedupe for Spacedust gaps
description: Spacedust has no replay. On reconnect, enumerate posts that referenced the Mage during the offline gap via Constellation backlink index (/links?target=<MAGE_DID>&collection=app.bsky.feed.post&path=.reply.parent.uri and the mention path), replay them through listeners, and dedupe by idempotency key (source_record+source_rev) via tass-job so commands never double-act. Jetstream source already replays via cursor, so this is Spacedust-only.

title: meditate handler (MVP v1.1 second flow)
description: Second and final MVP v1.1 flow. Keyword-spot 'meditate' -> resolve character + target Node -> write a com.superbfowle.tass.meditate record (node at-uri, amount 0-20). Deferred completion (effect after in-fiction duration) modeled as a tass-job due job. Shares tass-target-resolve and the action-chain runtime.

title: burn-tass handler (primary command)
description: Primary command. Keyword-spot 'burn (my) tass' in a post at us -> resolve character + tass (tass-target-resolve) -> authorize (user may only burn their OWN tass) -> write enervate in the Mage repo against the user's tassilize (enervate implies the user as effect target for now; recipient allocation is future, tass-recipient-alloc) -> write attestation (tass-attest-age-payload) -> optional reply (writes=all). Idempotent via tass-job.

title: Resolve character + tass from message words (multi-mage, multi-tass)
description: Shared resolution used by command handlers. CHARACTER: a user may own multiple mage records (actor.rpg.stats rkeys); pick the one whose character-name word appears in the message (the multiple-mage problem). TASS: match a tass form/name word in the message; if unspecified, pick a random owned tass. Keyword/name matching, deterministic-enough, with clear no-match diagnostics.

title: Wide-event tracing + per-listener verbosity filtering
description: One canonical wide-event log line per processed event at INFO (source, listener, matched, actor, target, steps, effect_uris, dry_run, dedupe_key, latency, outcome), assembled across a per-listener span. verbosity is a per-listener filter directive (EnvFilter span-field scoping, e.g. tassle::listener[{listener=burn-tass}]=debug) controlling which child step-spans print; per-layer filters let different sinks show different subsystems. Optional tracing-subscriber reload for live verbosity changes.

title: Two-level listener config: sources + per-listener knobs
description: Config schema: [sources.<name>] (enabled, endpoint, account) toggles a whole feed; [listeners.<name>] (source, reads, writes, verbosity, match params) independently enables/tunes each reaction. Global defaults + per-listener override. Spacedust endpoint configurable (public default). Integrates with the figment2 profile layering in tassle-config.

title: Listener engine: registry + keyword matcher + action-chain runtime + knobs
description: Core library. A LISTENER = {name, source, matcher, action_chain, reads, writes, verbosity} bound to a SOURCE. Registry dispatches each normalized event to matching listeners. Matcher = keyword spotting. Action chain = ordered steps that run/skip/short-circuit. Three orthogonal per-listener knobs: reads(off|on), writes(off|own|all), verbosity. dry-run emerges as reads=on,writes=off. Chains static-in-code for v1.1, shaped to grow into a named-step registry. Mage writes lend tassle-config AuthedClient/SessionSource (borrow model).

title: Shared fjall StoreProvider via jac-store-fjall builders
description: A StoreProvider handing out fjall partition/keyspace handles to sessions, ledger, and jobs from one data root, using jac-store-fjall's new DB builders (the instance creation formerly internal). Hydrant gone => all consumers resolve fjall from crates.io and stay version-aligned, so a single shared keyspace with handle-passing is straightforward (no version reconciliation).

title: Reusable durable job queue + worker (fjall, idempotent)
description: Extract the durable due-job mechanism into its own crate. Job = {inputUri, inputCid, kind, dueAt, status, attempts}; enactment idempotent by inputUri+inputCid+effectKind. Worker enacts at dueAt with retry/backoff. Backed by jac-store-fjall DB builders. Doubles as the dedupe ledger for backfilled/replayed events. Reusable beyond the listener (deferred meditate completion, future node regen).

title: Jetstream EventSource: tass-at-large firehose with cursor replay
description: Jetstream consumer impl of EventSource for com.superbfowle.tass.* records network-wide (wantedCollections). Full record bodies, cursor-bearing with replay on reconnect; advance cursor only after durable fjall commit. Feeds tass-ledger-fold. Replaces the deleted hydrant path; same jetstream crate Spacedust/Slingshot use.

title: Spacedust EventSource: WS subscribe + hydration adapter
description: Spacedust source impl of the EventSource trait. Connect WS /subscribe with wantedSubjectDids=<MAGE_DID> (+ optional wantedSources for post mention/reply paths); handle pings, reconnect, live filter-update frames, and slow-consumer backpressure. Payload is a pointer (source_record/source_rev/subject), so hydrate source_record via Slingshot getRecordByUri (PDS fallback) into the envelope body. Default delayed (21s) stream, not instant. Endpoint configurable; public spacedust.microcosm.blue default (confirm host).

title: enervate target routing (recipient Tasses, Nodes, recipient Mages)
description:
enervate today drains a Tass into the enervate author's pattern (the Mage). There is no explicit recipient field — authorship is the recipient model (see doc/discovery/spacedust.md open question 1, doc/design.gpt.md). We want enervate (or a wrapping record) to be able to route the drained quintessence to further targets beyond 'the author's pattern'.

TARGETS WE WANT:
- author's pattern (current default),
- a named Tass (move quintessence Tass-to-Tass),
- a Node's ambient pool (return current to a Node),
- a named recipient Mage (explicit recipient DID, addressed by the author).

DESIGN NOTES:
- Pluggable targets, with 'author pattern' as the default (preserves current semantics — enervate records that omit the routing field stay backward-compatible).
- The routing field can live on the enervate record itself (lexicon change, see tass-lex-codegen) or on a wrapping attestation record (see doc/discovery/lex-layers-pub.md on the changelog-shaped action records and doc/discovery/attestation.md). Probably a small lexicon extension that adds an optional recipient + recipientType (pattern | tass | node | mage) pair, where absent = author-pattern default.
- This is the natural home for the policy layer: the enervate consumer asks the policy 'is this transfer allowed / how much can land in this target?'
  - pattern target: Avatar-rated cap (see tass-quint-avatar-cap).
  - Node target: ambient-pool cap (rating * 5; lazy-mint semantics — see doc/discovery/lex-co-core.md weeklyRefresh / use-it-to-keep-it pattern).
  - Tass target: remaining-capacity (Tass max vs current).
  - mage recipient: same as pattern, but paternalistic — the author needs the recipient's sheet writable or needs to write an attestation the recipient's tools will honor.
- Targets should be pluggable so a chronicle can introduce new sinks (e.g. a caern, an artifact) without changing the lexicon each time.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A lexicon change (or a wrapping attestation record) that names a recipient + recipient-type on enervate, defaulting to author-pattern when absent.
- A TargetPolicy/trait with impls for: AuthorPattern, NamedTass, NodeAmbient, RecipientMage.
- Each target policy enforces its own cap (pattern -> Avatar cap, node -> ambient-pool cap, tass -> remaining capacity) and returns an allowed-amount + overflow.
- Backward-compat: an enervate record with no recipient field routes to the author's pattern exactly as today.
- Tests cover each target type and the cap behavior at the boundary.

title: Stale-quintessence detection + refresh
description:
Inline coherence check on the existing `QuintClient` read/write paths. There is NO new sync API / sync command / sync pass — the staleness check runs inside `read`, `write_with`, and `adjust_with` as part of doing what they already do. Callers keep calling the same methods; out comes a coherent value (or a coherent write).

DETECTION (default policy: milli is truth). The check fires inside `QuintClient::read` after the record is fetched, and inside `write_with`/`adjust_with` before the patch is built. A sheet is out of sync when EITHER:
  - quintessence != floor(milliQuintessence / 1000) (the displayed floor drifted away from the milli balance), OR
  - the record-level updatedAt is newer than milliQuintessenceUpdatedAt AND the write was not performed through tass-quint (no matching replication). The latter is the signal that something mutated the mage block outside the tass-quint writer (manual edit, older tool, third-party).

INLINE BEHAVIOR:
- On `read`: if drift detected, the returned `Quint` is the refreshed value per the active `SyncDirection` (default: re-replicate the floor — i.e. the read returns what the milli says; the sheet is NOT mutated by the read path itself, but the read value is the source-of-truth one). Optionally a stale flag/note surfaces that drift was found. A read MUST NOT write to the sheet.
- On `write_with`/`adjust_with`: the outgoing patch is checked/built so the persisted record satisfies the sync-direction invariant. Default (milli-is-truth): `sheet_patch` continues to write the floor into quintessence. QuintessenceIsTruth direction (see tass-quint-sync-direction): the patch hydrates milli from the requested quintessence points. Either way the write leaves the sheet consistent by construction.
- No new entry point. The `SyncPolicy`/`Coherence` trait (or fn) is a *seam* so the direction (and future drift rules) is pluggable, but its only callers are inside the existing quint library methods — nothing outside the crate calls it directly.

The check is cheap (compare two timestamps + two ints inline in the RMW).

Wires: child of tass-quint-policy. Tightly coupled to tass-quint-sync-direction (it decides which field the refresh honors). Sibling of tass-quint-avatar-cap.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A SyncPolicy / Coherence seam (trait or fn operating on the raw mage-block fields: milliQuintessence, quintessence, milliQuintessenceUpdatedAt, updatedAt) returning a SyncDecision { in_sync | refresh_floor | hydrate_milli }.
- Default impl detects the two drift cases above and returns refresh_floor.
- `QuintClient::read` returns the coherent `Quint` per the active direction. It does NOT issue a write. Drift is observable to the caller (stale flag or returned alongside the Quint).
- `QuintClient::write_with`/`adjust_with` build patches that leave the sheet coherent per the active direction (today's milli-is-truth path stays exactly as it is).
- Tests cover: in-sync sheet (no-op on read, normal write), floor drift on read (returns coherent value), updatedAt-only drift on read (returns coherent value), legacy-only sheet on read (hydrate-from-quintessence already handled by resolve()).
- No new sync* exported API on `QuintClient`. The Coherence seam is a pluggable impl, not a call site expansion.
- milliQuintessence and milliQuintessenceUpdatedAt are never altered by the milli-is-truth refresh path.
notes:
Implemented in tass-quint + tass-quint-jac.

LANDED:
- tass-quint: SyncDecision { InSync, RefreshFloor, HydrateMilli }, SheetFields, Coherence trait, MilliIsTruthCoherence default impl (detects floor drift + timestamp drift, both -> RefreshFloor; never returns HydrateMilli), ReadReport { quint, decision } + in_sync()/drifted() helpers, coherent_quint(fields, decision) helper. 10 unit tests.
- tass-quint-jac: extract_fields(Value) -> SheetFields helper; QuintClient::read now routes through read_report and returns the coherent Quint (drift silent); QuintClient::read_report returns Option<ReadReport> for drift observability; write_with/adjust_with unchanged (sheet_patch already replicates the floor = the milli-is-truth coherent action). Pluggability: QuintClient gains a Co: Coherence = MilliIsTruthCoherence type param + with_coherence() constructor + coherence() accessor; the default new() path is unaffected. 8 new tests including a custom AlwaysHydrateFromLegacy impl proving the seam routes HydrateMilli end-to-end.
- doc/microquint.md: noted the read_report variant.

Scope kept tight: no SyncDirection enum, no QuintessenceIsTruth branch (sibling tass-quint-sync-direction territory — flagged as possible miscommunication, TBD). The HydrateMilli variant + coherent_quint's hydrate-from-legacy branch are forward-compat for whichever direction we settle on.

VERIFIED: cargo test -p tass-quint (24 tests), cargo test -p tass-quint-jac (22 tests + 3 doctests), cargo clippy --all-targets clean, cargo check --workspace clean.

title: Quintessence sync/coherence policy + enervate target routing
description:
Now that we track `milliQuintessenceUpdatedAt`, the existing quint library (`QuintClient` read/write/adjust) needs an inline *policy seam* that decides which field is the system of record and what to do when the two fields (or their timestamps) disagree. Today tass-quint hardcodes milli-is-truth with the legacy quintessence as a derived floor (replicated on every write). We want that decision configurable per chronicle/reality without inventing a separate sync pass — the policies are co-located with the read and write calls themselves.

DEFAULT POLICY: milliQuintessence is the system of record. Reads use it directly; the legacy quintessence field is a replicated display value. This is the current behavior and stays the default.

POLICIES WE WANT (all inline in QuintClient; no new sync API):

1. Stale-quintessence detection + refresh (see tass-quint-stale-sync). The read path returns a value coherent with the active sync direction; the write path leaves the sheet coherent by construction. No separate sync verb.
2. Avatar-cap enforcement on adjustment (see tass-quint-avatar-cap). Inline in `write_with`/`adjust_with` behind an opt-in WriteOpts/policy option.
3. Sync direction selector (see tass-quint-sync-direction). `enum SyncDirection { MilliIsTruth, QuintessenceIsTruth }`, default MilliIsTruth. The stale-refresh action (1) and the write-patch construction both honor it.

ENERVATE TARGET ROUTING (see tass-enervate-targets):

enervate today drains a Tass into the enervate author's pattern (the Mage). There is no explicit recipient field — authorship is the recipient model (see doc/discovery/spacedust.md open question 1). We want enervate (or a wrapping record) to be able to route the drained quintessence to further targets beyond 'the author's pattern':
   - the author's pattern (current default),
   - a named Tass (move quintessence Tass-to-Tass),
   - a Node's ambient pool (return current to a Node),
   - a named recipient Mage (explicit recipient DID, addressed by the author).
Pluggable targets, with 'author pattern' as the default. This is the natural home for the policy layer too: the enervate consumer asks the policy 'is this transfer allowed / how much can land in this target' (Avatar cap for a pattern target, ambient-pool cap for a Node target, remaining-capacity for a Tass target).

title: tassle quint set|inc CLI + QuintClient ergonomics
description:
Tracks the tassle quint set/inc write commands and the QuintClient ergonomics that support them.

LANDED:
- tass-quint: Quint::from_points_f64 (rounds a points float to millis; saturates on non-finite) + Quint::add_millis (saturating i64 add). Makes 'set 3.5' / 'inc -0.25' ergonomic at the math layer.
- tass-quint-jac: QuintClient::adjust / adjust_with (read-modify-write) powering 'inc'; WriteOpts (.at/.unstamped) already there from the stamping-default work.
- tassle-config: AuthedClient::for_profile(cli_override) so quint honours --profile.
- tassle-cli: 'tassle quint set <points>' and 'tassle quint inc [points=+1.0]' behind the auth-store feature, wiring AuthedClient::for_profile -> &session -> QuintClient::new(&session) -> write_with/adjust_with (the borrow model). Float input finiteness-checked; out-of-range results warn. Optional --at/--unstamped on both.

ERGONOMICS ASSESSMENT: the flows 'just work' through QuintClient now. One residual friction: repo+rkey ('mage') is passed on every call (acceptable for a command that captures them once as locals). A future bound handle (e.g. AuthedClient::quint_client() returning a repo+rkey-bound QuintClient view) could remove that repetition — not blocking.

NOT DONE (follow-ups):
- Integration test against a real PDS exercising AuthedClient -> QuintClient write INCLUDING a refresh cycle (validates the borrow model end-to-end and the residual single-session concurrent-refresh edge).
- inc atomicity: adjust is read-modify-write (separate get/put), unsafe under concurrent writers. Add optimistic concurrency via swapRecord/swapCommit when concurrency matters.
- Bound handle to reduce repo+rkey repetition (see ergonomics note above).
acceptance_criteria: quint set/inc commands exist behind auth-store and round-trip milliQuintessence on a logged-in profile's actor.rpg.stats/mage sheet (set absolute, inc signed delta, optional --at/--unstamped, --profile honoured). Float math tested in tass-quint. Integration test against a PDS (incl. a forced refresh) lands to validate end-to-end. Documented concurrency contract for inc (non-atomic until swapRecord).
notes: Commits: feat(quint): float-aware constructors; feat(quint-jac): adjust/adjust_with; feat(config): AuthedClient::for_profile; feat(cli): tassle quint set|inc. Uses the borrow model decided under tass-config-session-source. Related: tass-quint-auth-config (the AuthedClient path), tass-config-session-source (refresh hazard / borrow model).

title: AGE-encrypted before→after payload on attestation records
description: When the Spacedust command listener (see doc/discovery/spacedust.md) performs an action for a user — enervate on 'burn my tass', meditate on 'meditate' — it posts an attestation record in our own NSID (com.superbfowle.tass.*) saying we did the thing. Current attestation ideas are weak. This issue adds a verifiable, privacy-preserving state-change record: attach an AGE-encrypted payload to the attestation that captures exactly what changed, from -> to (e.g. the affected tass quintessence before and after the enervate, or the Mage pattern total before/after a meditate), without publishing raw ledger numbers to the public firehose. The affected party (key holder) can decrypt and verify; everyone else sees only an opaque blob.
design: Use AGE (age-encryption.org) for the encrypted payload — likely the rage Rust crate. Encrypt a small canonical JSON {action_uri, target_uri, field, from, to, at} to one or more AGE recipients. Open: recipient/key discovery — does the affected user publish an AGE public key (e.g. in a tass profile record or did-doc-adjacent record), or do we encrypt to the reality's key? Where the ciphertext lives: an opaque field on the attestation record (base64/) vs. a blob ref. Distinct from doc/discovery/attestation.md, which is about cosign TRUST (keytrace et al.); this is a lightweight action RECEIPT with a confidential diff. Relates to the listener/attestation step in doc/discovery/spacedust.md.
notes: Filed from the spacedust discovery session. Primary action is enervate ('burn my tass' -> quintessence to the Mage); meditate is the only other MVP v1.1 flow.

title: SessionSource (cloneable session factory) — spike + refresh-correctness hazard
description:
Tracks the SessionSource spike landed in tassle-config (behind auth-store) and the serious concurrency hazard it exposes.

WHAT LANDED: SessionSource is a Clone factory that vends owned CredentialSessions over a shared Arc store+resolver, keyed by SessionKey, pointed at a PDS. AuthedClient wraps it and is now Clone. Spawning is cheap because CredentialSession::access_token() reads the bearer token from the shared SessionStore by key (credential_session.rs:313), so a fresh session authenticates with no re-resume/no network.

THE HAZARD (the real point of this ticket): CredentialSession::refresh() (credential_session.rs:333-366) reads the refresh JWT from the SHARED store, POSTs com.atproto.server.refreshSession, and writes the new session back to the store — with NO coordination across CredentialSession instances (send_with_opts at :686-716 triggers refresh on 401/expiry, also uncoordinated). ATProto rotates refresh JWTs: each refresh invalidates the prior refresh JWT (with only a brief grace window). So multiple CONCURRENT sessions vended by one SessionSource that hit 401 simultaneously will each fire refreshSession, racing on the rotating refresh JWT — one can invalidate the other's refresh JWT, and last-writer-wins store.set can leave an orphaned/stale refresh JWT so the next refresh fails -> session dies -> re-login required.

So SessionSource-vends-many is UNSAFE for concurrent use today. It is only safe when sessions are used SERIALLY (one live CredentialSession at a time). Refresh percolates THROUGH THE SHARED STORE (token state is consistent), but the refresh ACT does not percolate 'up to' SessionSource — SessionSource is a dumb factory with no refresh state or visibility.

DECISION NEEDED: (a) single-session model — borrow or a serializing closure-lease (with_session holds an internal async mutex, lends one session at a time), no race, re-prioritize this; (b) upstream refresh coordination in jacquard (serialize + coalesce refreshes per session key) — see tass-refresh-coordination — after which multi-session is genuinely safe.

ALSO: SessionSource relies on the undocumented jacquard behavior that access_token()/refresh read from the store; if that ever changes, spawned sessions silently go stale.
acceptance_criteria: Pick ONE safe session model and encode it (likely single-session borrow, or a serializing lease via with_session), OR explicitly block on tass-refresh-coordination. Add an integration test that exercises AuthedClient -> SessionSource -> a real write INCLUDING a refresh cycle, proving the chosen model does not race. Document the concurrency contract clearly on SessionSource/AuthedClient (e.g. 'vend at most one live session at a time until tass-refresh-coordination lands').
notes:
Spike commits: 'spike(config): add tassle-config crate' + 'spike(config): add cloneable SessionSource; make AuthedClient Clone'. Refresh analysis grounded in jacquard 0.12.1 credential_session.rs:313 (access_token store-backed), :333-366 (refresh), :686-716 (send_with_opts, uncoordinated refresh trigger). Strongly overlaps tass-refresh-coordination (the real fix lives upstream). The borrow model (QuintClient holds &C) sidesteps this entirely by using one session.
Decision (2026-06-30): moved to the BORROW model. QuintClient<'c, C> now holds &'c C; AuthedClient holds ONE live CredentialSession and lends it via session() -> &AppPasswordSession. SessionSource (the cloneable factory) was REMOVED as the rejected owned-clone path. Borrow = one session shared by &self = the safe concurrency model (jacquard's send(&self) is built for it); does NOT serialize to one-at-a-time (many concurrent QuintClient uses share the borrowed session). This sidesteps the multi-instance refresh escalation but NOT the single-instance concurrent-refresh edge.

Filed the upstream issue asking jacquard to fix the root cause: doc/upstream/jacquard-refresh-coordination.md (ready-to-paste; proposes single-flight refresh keyed by SessionKey, notes the Arc<Rwlock<_>>+Clone nice-to-have). Commits: 'feat(quint): move to the borrow model' + 'doc(upstream): jacquard CredentialSession refresh-coordination issue'.

Ticket stays open until upstream lands (then we could revisit a clonable handle) OR can close once the borrow model is wired end-to-end (tassle quint set) with an integration test.

title: Pave a clean path to an authenticated XrpcClient for write paths
description: Write paths (tass-quint-jac QuintClient::write, a future 'tassle quint set', the meditate/enervate sheet-patch flows) need an authenticated jacquard client. Today the only authed-client construction lives inline in crates/tassle-cli/src/commands/auth.rs behind the 'auth-store' cargo feature: FjallAuth::open(path)?.app_password() -> CredentialSession::new(store, resolver), then session.resume(SessionHint). profile_config.rs exposes profile metadata (DID/handle/PDS via default_profile/default_did) but NO client. There is no 'give me the active profile's authed XrpcClient' helper, so every write command re-derives the dance. Figure out how to pave this nicely: a single, well-named constructor/path that resolves active profile -> PDS -> authed client (token refresh handled by CredentialSession), reusable by any write command, gated so read-only builds don't pull auth deps.
acceptance_criteria: A documented, reusable path to obtain an authed XrpcClient (pointed at the actor's PDS) from the active profile — e.g. a helper in tassle-cli or the tassle-app Runtime (see tass-app-crate). tass-quint-jac QuintClient::write is exercised end-to-end (or via a 'tassle quint set'/sheet-patch command) using that path. Read-only/default builds are not forced to compile jac-store-fjall/rpassword. The path handles (or delegates) token refresh + session resume so callers don't re-implement auth.rs. The authed-client construction currently inline in auth.rs is factored out behind the new path.
notes:
User flagged directly: 'it's not clear to me what paths we have for auth config to be easily gotten to — this is probably something we need to figure out how to pave nicely and make good.' Likely broader than quint (Runtime/app-layer concern) but the quint write path is the first concrete consumer. Relevant refs: crates/tassle-cli/src/commands/auth.rs (CredentialSession construction), crates/tassle-cli/src/profile_config.rs (default_profile, default_did — metadata only), tassle-cli auth-store feature.
Design sketch (2026-06-30):

GAP: only authed-client construction is inline in auth.rs::login_real (lines 362-386: FjallAuth::open -> app_password -> CredentialSession::new -> resume). profile_config.rs exposes metadata only (Profile{did,handle,pds}, default_profile/default_did) — NO client. Reads (mage list: BasicClient::unauthenticated + resolve_actor/set_base_uri) and writes (login: CredentialSession) take totally different paths. Two profile-fragment locations (config.toml.d/<did>.toml vs dropins_dir) compound the confusion.

PROPOSED SHAPE: a small AuthedClient helper behind an 'authed-client' cargo feature in tassle-cli (the home until tassle-app/tass-app-crate lands). AuthedClient::for_active_profile() does: resolve active profile via figment -> FjallAuth::open(store_path)?.app_password() -> CredentialSession::new(store, resolver) -> session.resume(hint) (LoginRequired => error 'run tassle auth login', never prompt) -> set_base_uri(pds). Exposes did() + session() (an XrpcClient+Sync with base_uri already pointed). Write command becomes: let c = AuthedClient::for_active_profile().await?; QuintClient::new(c.session()).write(c.did(), "mage", q).await?. tass-quint-jac stays client-agnostic — helper is a tassle-cli/app concern.

FEATURE GATING: keep 'auth-store' (jac-store-fjall/rpassword/base64, low-level); add 'authed-client' feature that implies auth-store and gates just the helper. Reads stay off; write paths turn on authed-client. Collapse to one feature if no consumer wants store-without-helper.

GENERAL AUTH WORK (this ticket is a slice of a bigger epic): (1) unify read+write client acquisition into one Client::for_profile(p, authed) that always handles PDS pointing (today copy-pasted in mage.rs::resolve_actor); (2) one profile source of truth — reconcile config.toml.d vs dropins_dir; (3) a tassle-app Runtime (tass-app-crate) owning active-profile+client+config — the real home for AuthedClient; (4) scope assertion — writes need repo:actor.rpg.stats (src/auth/scopes.ts:12), helper should verify the resumed session carries it.

title: Pluggable quintessence resolution algorithms for tass-quint
description: Today tass-quint prescribes a single resolution algorithm: Quint::points() = millis / 1000 (floor), and resolve() prefers milliQuintessence then hydrates from quintessence*1000. We explicitly do NOT want to prescribe one algorithm — different chronicles/realities may derive the displayed whole-point quintessence from the milli balance differently (floor today; later rounding, threshold, decay, etc.). Make resolution pluggable behind a trait so the read path (mage list, QuintClient::read) and the sheet_patch replication can be driven by a chosen resolver, while the milliQuintessence source-of-truth + milliQuintessenceUpdatedAt tracking stays fixed and algorithm-agnostic.
acceptance_criteria: A Resolve/QuintResolver trait in tass-quint abstracts whole-point derivation + hydration, with Floor as the default impl (current behavior). resolve() and sheet_patch() (or successors) accept/use a resolver so callers can plug alternatives. Existing floor behavior preserved as the default; tests cover Floor plus at least one alternative (e.g. Round) to prove extensibility. mage list and QuintClient::read stay resolver-agnostic (default Floor) unless a caller opts in. The milliQuintessence value and milliQuintessenceUpdatedAt tracking are never altered by the resolver choice.
notes: Seed already landed: feat(quint): tass-quint core crate + feat(quint): track milliQuintessenceUpdatedAt on writes. The floor algorithm and tracking are universal; this ticket only makes derivation pluggable.

title: Token refresh — CLI + autonomic use
description: Token refresh for app-password (MVP) and OAuth (later), for both the short-lived CLI and long-running/autonomic use (web service, hydrant listener, daemons). The loopback port is NOT involved in refresh (login-time only; skyboard's port-persistence bug does NOT apply to Jacquard — see cli-surface-findings §4.4), so we store NO port. Refresh is a direct token-endpoint call (refreshSession / OAuth refresh) using stored tokens (+ DPoP key for OAuth). jacquard CredentialSession already does reactive refresh on TokenExpired/401 and persists (credential_session.rs:333,686,705); OAuthSession adds proactive pre-expiry + in-process coalescing via SessionRegistry (oauth/session.rs:432). Children scope CLI verification + the autonomic gaps.

title: generate to publish bridge (mint parity)
description:
Restore `mint` parity (`src/commands/mint.ts` - build + publish a Node) on the Rust side as a generate->publish bridge. `tassle generate` produces a validated record locally; this adds the "write it" half: take a factory-built record, resolve the active profile/PDS via `tass-app`, and put it to the actor repo under the right collection.

Keeps the existing split: `generate` = construct locally (no auth needed); publish = a separate step that needs a session. Could surface as `generate <kind> --publish`, a `put`/`create` command, or both - the design decides.

## Depends on

- `tass-record-factory` - construction.
- `tass-auth-mvp` - app-password session to write with.

## Related

- `tass-xrpc-surface` - the `tassilize` procedure sketch covers source-kind distinction.
acceptance_criteria: A factory-built record can be published to the active profile repo from the CLI. generate without publish is unchanged (no auth required). Publish path goes through tass-app / tass-factory, not inline construction. Rkey strategy documented (TID default, per doc/design.gpt.md:280).

title: generate CLI for all record types (tassilize/meditate/enervate/form/resonance)
description:
Extend `tassle generate` (`crates/tassle-cli/src/commands/generate/`) beyond `node` to cover every `com.superbfowle.tass.*` record type: `tassilize`, `meditate`, `enervate`, and (once authored) `form`, `resonance`. Each subcommand is a thin wrapper: clap args -> `tass-factory` constructor -> validate -> `Render`. No construction logic in the CLI; the factory owns it.

Today only `generate node` exists (`generate/mod.rs:17`). The TS reference for each record field set is in `src/atproto/tass.ts`.

## Depends on

- `tass-record-factory` - construction.
- `tass-output-layer` - emit.

## XRPC parity

Each new subcommand gets a WIP XRPC sketch per `tass-xrpc-surface` (the `tassilize` procedure already has a source-kind distinction captured there).
acceptance_criteria: generate tassilize|meditate|enervate subcommands matching the TS field sets in src/atproto/tass.ts. Each uses tass-record-factory for construction (no inline defaults/validation). Each emits through the shared output layer (tass-output-layer). --help text consistent across subcommands; required vs optional flags clear. Each subcommand has at least one end-to-end test (build -> validate -> json output).

title: Output layer: Render trait + record envelope (json/cbor/table)
description:
Unify the three current formatting paths into one Render layer:

1. `OutputFormat` (json/cbor) + `emit()` in `crates/tassle-cli/src/commands/mod.rs:18,27` - record-only.
2. The global `--format json|table` dispatch tracked by `tass-cli-format-flag` (currently a child of `tass-auth-mvp`).
3. Ad-hoc `println!` human output in mage/repo/auth/config (e.g. `mage.rs:361`, `auth.rs:96`, `config.rs:50`).

Deliver a `Render` trait plus the record envelope from the README (`{uri,cid,repo,collection,rkey,value,normalized}`): one place that renders a record or record list as json, dag-cbor, or a human table. Record-producing commands (`generate`, `samples`) and read commands (`repo`, `mage`) both go through it.

## Scope this pass

**Trait + envelope only.** Per-command human table formatting stays as-is for now; this ticket provides the shared trait and envelope type and migrates record `emit()` plus the global `--format` dispatch onto it. Full table-renderer consolidation (retiring the scattered println!) is a follow-up.

## Related

- Extends `tass-cli-format-flag`; consider lifting that ticket out of `tass-auth-mvp` into `tass-build-render` once the trait lands.
acceptance_criteria: A Render trait (or equivalent) with json/cbor/table arms and an envelope type matching the README {uri,cid,repo,collection,rkey,value,normalized} shape. generate and samples emit through the trait (replacing commands::emit). Global --format dispatch routes through the trait. No regression in existing json output (byte-equivalent for records). Envelope documented with a table or mermaid in the design doc.
notes: Design sketch: doc/generate-design.md#2-output-layer

title: Move sample definitions out of CLI into corpus/factory presets
description:
Relocate the canonical sample definitions out of `crates/tassle-cli/src/commands/samples.rs:53` (`build_samples`) so the CLI `samples` command is a thin caller. Today the CLI owns both the illustrative values and the write loop; the corpus (`crates/tass-lex-corpus`) only embeds the generated output behind its `samples` feature (`Cargo.toml:15`). Definitions should live next to the corpus - either as data exposed by `tass-lex-corpus`, or as named presets in the new `tass-factory` crate - so tests, docs, and the CLI all cite one source.

## Scope this pass

**Relocate only.** Keep hand-authored sample values; do not pursue schema-driven synthesis (the README "true lexicon-driven generator" stays deferred). The `samples` feature flag in `tass-lex-corpus` already exists and stays as-is.

## Depends on

- `tass-record-factory` - if presets live in the factory crate.
acceptance_criteria: samples.rs build_samples no longer holds the sample values; it calls a corpus/factory API. Existing four sample files regenerate byte-identically (fixed createdAt preserved). Corpus samples feature still gates embedded output; no behavior change for consumers with default features off. samples --dry-run still lists filenames + descriptions.
notes: Implemented via a sample crate + xtask rather than factory presets. Created tass-lex-sample (embedded fixture JSON, zero-dep, mirrors the schema crate shape) and an xtask workspace crate with `cargo xtask samples` (moved build_samples out of the CLI; removed the tassle samples CLI command). Byte-identical regen verified (git diff on the sample dir empty after regen). The sample crate is a committed snapshot of builder output; when tass-record-factory lands, the xtask should switch from calling generated builders directly to calling tass_factory::samples presets — that wiring is the remaining follow-up. --dry-run preserved as `cargo xtask samples --dry-run`.

title: Record factory crate: defaults/derived/semantic facade over tass-lex builders
description:
New `tass-factory` crate providing a hand-written facade over the jacquard-generated `tass-lex` builders. The generated builders (type-state, in `crates/tassle-lexicons`) intentionally have no defaults and no cross-field rules - they only enforce the lexicon schema. The factory owns:

- **Seed defaults** - e.g. `ambientQuintessence` defaults to `rating * 5` when unset (restoring `src/atproto/tass.ts:123` behavior lost in the port; currently `generate node` emits no `ambientQuintessence` when the flag is omitted - `crates/tassle-cli/src/commands/generate/node.rs:67-69`).
- **Semantic validation** - vocabulary checks the schema cannot express (resonance values, form references), beyond `LexiconSchema::validate`.
- **Cross-field rules** - relationships the builder cannot encode (e.g. tassilize quintessence bounded by node rating).
- **Option-passthrough ergonomics** - so CLI `Option<T>` flows straight through without the `maybe_*(Some(...))` dance.

This is the "generate is also just a builder thing generally" layer: a library the CLI, ledger, tests, and future web/XRPC all import, so there is exactly one place that knows how to construct a valid Tass record.

## Out of scope

Derived truth (ledger recomputes `ambientQuintessence` from action history per `doc/discovery/lexicon-ideas.md:193`). The factory only produces the asserted seed.

## Related

- `tass-lex-codegen` / `tass-crate-rename` - naming/cleanup; the new crate is born `tass-*` regardless of rename order.
acceptance_criteria: New crates/tass-factory crate with constructors for node/tassilize/meditate/enervate (form/resonance as they land). ambientQuintessence defaults to rating*5 when unset, overridable. Option-passthrough setters accept T or None and skip on None. generate node migrates to the factory; output unchanged when defaults apply. Unit tests cover defaults, overrides, and semantic validation failures. Crate compiles with no warnings; consumed by tassle-cli.
notes: Design sketch: doc/generate-design.md#1-tass-factory

title: [epic] Record construct+render pipeline (port TS generate to Rust; unify output)
description:
Port the legacy TypeScript `mint`/`generate` path into a layered Rust **construct -> render** pipeline that is reusable beyond the CLI. Today the Rust side has only `generate node`, hand-coded sample definitions living inside the CLI command, and output split between a record-only `OutputFormat` (json/cbor) and ad-hoc `println!` human formatting scattered across mage/repo/auth/config. Meanwhile the TS builders carried real construction policy - derived defaults like `ambientQuintessence = rating * 5`, range validation, optional-skip ergonomics - that the jacquard-generated type-state builders do not reproduce.

## Why this epic

This epic ties those threads into one pipeline:

1. **Factory** - a hand-written facade over generated builders that owns seed defaults, semantic validation, and cross-field rules. Lives in a new reusable crate (`tass-factory`) so the CLI, the ledger fold, tests, and the future web/XRPC surface share one construction path.
2. **Generate CLI** - per-record subcommands that are thin wrappers over factory + render.
3. **Samples** - canonical example records defined alongside the corpus, not inside the CLI.
4. **Output** - a Render trait plus the `{uri,cid,repo,collection,rkey,value,normalized}` envelope, unifying the record `OutputFormat` with the global `--format json|table` dispatch.
5. **Publish** - a generate->write bridge restoring `mint` parity once app-password auth lands.

Every read/write command and every future XRPC method (`tass-xrpc-surface`) converges on the same record shape and the same construction rules. Building the factory + render once, as library crates, prevents three parallel copies of "how to build and print a Node".

## Scope boundary

Derived *truth* (e.g. recomputing `ambientQuintessence` from action history) belongs to the ledger fold (`tass-ledger-fold`), not this epic. The factory only produces the **asserted seed record**; the ledger reconciles it.

## Related (linked, not duplicated)

- `tass-lex-codegen` / `tass-crate-rename` - layer 1 builder/codegen cleanup the factory builds on.
- `tass-cli-format-flag` - absorbed/extended by `tass-output-layer`; consider lifting into this epic.
- `tass-xrpc-surface` - every factory/generate op gets an XRPC sketch.
- `tass-auth-mvp` - gates `tass-generate-publish`.
notes: Early design draft: doc/generate-design.md (factory Spec API, Render trait + envelope, samples relocation, suggested ordering).

title: Design: config set writing into the figment profile + drop-in model
description:
Open design question flagged during tass-config-command-shape. config set key=value currently writes to the active profile's drop-in fragment (config.toml.d/<active>.toml), with the special key 'profile' redirected to base config.toml. That works for the common case but the interaction with figments-rs DropIns::profile_gated has unresolved edges we are deliberately NOT answering here — this ticket exists to call them out so we avoid/defer, and so any implementation that stumbles into them warns loudly.

Context / known edges:
- DropIns::profile_gated only loads a fragment whose stem matches the SELECTED (custom) profile. So writing to a fragment for a non-active profile is fine on disk but is NOT reflected until that profile is selected.
- If NO custom profile is selected (active = figment 'default'), profile_gated loads nothing — so writing to default.toml would silently not round-trip through config get. config set should warn or refuse in that state.
- The 'profile' selector key is special-cased to base config.toml; that is a bolt-on, not a first-class figment operation. Writing any other key to base config.toml (the Default-profile defaults) is not exposed at all.
- 'config set IN a named profile' (--profile X did=...) semantics are undefined: does it write X.toml directly, or switch active first? The drop-in-per-profile layout implies one file per profile, but imperative writes are awkward over a read-oriented, profile-gated loader.
- store_path (per-profile fjall store location) defaults to <xdg>/tassle/store/<profile>.fjall (XDG_CONFIG_HOME today; consider XDG_DATA_HOME for data-vs-config correctness — minor, note here). The LoginProfile field is named store_path.

Strategy: avoid/defer where possible. If auth-status / auth-switch / globals implementation trips over any of these, surface a warning and reference this ticket rather than silently papering over it. Resolve the semantics (likely: config set always targets the active custom profile's fragment; refuse when no custom profile selected; add a first-class 'config set --profile X' later) when we have a concrete need.

Supersedes nothing; related to tass-config-command-shape (closed), tass-cli-profile-flag, tass-auth-status.
notes: RESOLVED via option (c): Default is a real, always-loaded, low-priority fallback. Base config.toml loads non-nested into figment's Default profile (already the case); reads already fall back Default<custom (verified). The missing piece was write routing: config set now writes to base config.toml when the active profile is 'default' (no custom profile selected, or --profile default) OR the key is 'profile'; otherwise to config.toml.d/<active>.toml. So a Default write is a shared default inherited by every profile without its own setting, and a per-profile write overrides. Verified: --profile default config set did=... lands in base; storyteller (no own did) inherits it; player (own did) overrides; auth_mode set on Default is seen by player. Opt-out (a flag/env/config to disable Default fallback on reads) deferred — note if a concrete need arises. ANALOG (not fixed here): auth login_real writes did/handle to <profile_name>.toml; if profile_name=='default' that fragment won't load — login should target a named profile (covered by tass-auth-login follow-up).

title: Global --format json|table flag
description: Top-level --format json|table (default table) replacing per-command --json; migrate commands::emit and the auth/config commands to a global format dispatcher. Sets up --format cbor for later.
notes: Done. Global --format json|table|cbor (default table) added to the Cli struct as a clap global arg (accepted before or after the subcommand). OutputFormat extended to {Table, Json, Cbor} with is_json() helper; emit(Table) falls back to pretty JSON for single records (no tabular form). Threaded format into every command's run() and removed the per-command --json (auth login/status/set/switch, repo list, mage list, self stats) and generate node's --output — they all use the global format now. Verified: auth status/config list default table vs --format json; --format json works both before and after the subcommand; generate node default (pretty json) / --format json / --format cbor. repo/mage/self_record follow the same is_json() pattern (compile-clean; network-gated for live data).

title: Global --profile <name> flag
description: Top-level clap --profile <name> threaded into config::active_figment so every command honours the per-invocation profile override. Precedence: --profile > TASSLE_PROFILE > config file. Cross-cutting; touches the Cli struct and every command that loads the figment.
notes: Done. Global --profile <name> (clap global arg on Cli) threaded as Option<&str> into the figment-aware auth + config run()s and their fns (status, login_real, files, list, get, set); each now calls config::active_figment(profile) instead of active_figment(None). Precedence: --profile > TASSLE_PROFILE > config file 'profile' key (active_figment already does cli.map().or_else(env)). Verified: --profile storyteller overrides config's player for config list/get did + auth status active mark; --profile wins over TASSLE_PROFILE; unknown profile falls through to empty (fields missing). repo/mage/self_record still use the legacy default_did path (not figment) so --profile does not yet apply there — follows the legacy->figment migration (tass-app-crate / config-set-in-profile scope).

title: tassle auth switch
description: Set the active profile by writing profile = <name> to base config.toml (same as config set profile=), validate the fragment exists, no token work. Hidden top-level switch alias.
notes: Done. tassle auth switch <profile> validates the target has a config.toml.d/<name>.toml fragment (else errors with the available list), then writes profile = <name> to base config.toml via profile_config::write_value_at (same effect as 'config set profile=<name>' but with validation). No token work. Verified: switch changes active (confirmed by config list), unknown profile errors with available list, base config.toml updated. JSON + table output.

title: tassle auth logout
description: Revoke (deleteSession via jacquard) and remove the AtpSession row from the profile jac-store-fjall store. Default target = active profile; accept a profile or did arg; --all clears every stored session.

title: tassle auth status / whoami
description: Enumerate every profile (config.toml.d fragments) and, for each, look up its stored AtpSession in the profile jac-store-fjall store, decode the access_jwt exp claim, mark the active profile, show did/handle/pds/storage_path. Honour the global --format. Hidden top-level whoami alias.
notes: Done. tassle auth status lists every profile (config.toml.d fragments), marks the active one, shows did/handle/pds/resolved store_path, and under the auth-store feature opens each profile's FjallAuth store and looks up the AtpSession by SessionKey{did, session_id} (Did::new_owned + SessionKey::new + SessionStore::get), decoding the access_jwt exp via base64url+serde (state: ok/expired/absent, with ISO exp). Never creates empty stores (skips when the path is absent). Default build (no auth-store) shows 'session: n/a'. Verified: profiles listing + active marker + 'no session' (absent store) on auth-store build; JSON + table outputs; empty-profiles case. Session present/expired display is real-cred-gated (exercise via real login).

title: Real app-password auth login (replace ADR 0001 stub)
description: Replace the non-authenticating ADR 0001 auth login stub with real app-password auth: prompt for identifier+password (or accept flags/env), run CredentialSession resume_or_login (createSession), persist the AtpSession to the profile jac-store-fjall store, set the profile active. Followed by auth status / logout / switch. App-password only for MVP (OAuth deferred). --profile and --format json|table global flags threaded through.
notes:
Login verb landed (auth-store feature). auth login <actor> now does real app-password createSession: resolves --password > TASSLE_PASSWORD > rpassword prompt; opens the active profile's FjallAuth store (explicit storage_path or default config.toml.d/../store/<profile>.fjall); CredentialSession::new + resume(&SessionHint) and on LoginRequired calls login_from_challenge (createSession); jacquard persists the AtpSession JWTs into the fjall store automatically; the non-secret did/handle/pds/session_id land in config.toml.d/<profile>.toml via profile_config::write_value_at. Compile-verified for both feature configs; runtime-verified up to the network call (with a fake handle it reaches jacquard identity resolution and fails there as expected — needs real creds for a full createSession). Without auth-store, falls back to the ADR-0001 profile-only stub. REMAINING (not in this increment): auth status / logout / switch verbs; --profile and --format global flags threaded through account-scoped commands. Real-cred end-to-end verification pending.
Login verb delivered and compile/runtime-verified (reaches jacquard createSession). The remainder of this ticket — status/logout/switch verbs and the --profile/--format/--account globals — has been split into dedicated children: tass-auth-status, tass-auth-logout, tass-auth-switch, tass-cli-profile-flag, tass-cli-format-flag, tass-cli-account-flag. Closing this one for the login verb.

title: Extract crates/tassle-app with Runtime::from_profile bootstrap
description: Create a shared tassle-app library crate holding the domain actions (currently inlined in tassle-cli/src/commands) plus Runtime::from_profile(figment, profile): extract the active figment profile, boot that profile jac-store-fjall store, construct a jacquard CredentialSession, return an Agent. Both tassle-cli and a future web service consume this so auth wiring is neither stranded in the CLI nor wrapped in a confusing layer.

title: Auth spike: fjall store + CredentialSession resume on empty store
description: Throwaway spike proving the stack composes end to end: open a jac-store-fjall AppPasswordStore via its bon builder, construct a jacquard CredentialSession, call resume() against an empty store and observe LoginRequired. Confirms the post-cleanup bon builder surface and the 0.12 trait alignment before real plumbing. Lives in .test-agent/ or an example bin.
notes: Done. crates/tassle-cli/examples/auth_spike.rs opens a FjallAuth (Cbor) via FjallAuth::open(tempdir), wraps CredentialSession::new(Arc<app_password()>, Arc<JacquardResolver::default()>) and calls resume(SessionHint::any()). Compiled and RUN: empty store returns LoginRequired{identifier=None,session_id=None} without touching the network (Any hint short-circuits before resolver). Confirmed post-cleanup API: FjallAuth is the high-level handle with .app_password()/.oauth() accessors sharing one fjall DB; bon builder is FjallAuth::builder().path(p).codec(c).retry(r).harden(h).lock(l).build(). doc/auth-design.md §4.2 updated with the real surface.

title: Add jac-store-fjall as workspace path dependency
description: Add ~/src/jac-store-fjall as a path dependency of the tassle workspace, behind a cargo feature so the base CLI stays lean. Verify the unified build resolves jacquard to a single 0.12.x across both crates so the SessionStore/ClientAuthStore trait impls type-check against the same jacquard. Unblocks the auth spike.
notes: Done. Added jac-store-fjall as a workspace path dep (../jac-store-fjall), feature-gated in tassle-cli behind `auth-store` (optional dep) so the base CLI stays lean. cargo check -p tassle-cli clean both with and without --features auth-store; Cargo.lock unifies jacquard to a single 0.12.1 across tassle + jac-store-fjall (trait impls align). jac-store-fjall's hydrant fjall [patch.crates-io] does not cross the path boundary, so tassle resolves fjall from crates.io — fine for the auth MVP.

title: App-password auth MVP: jac-store-fjall sessions + figment profile switching
description: Land real app-password auth in tassle by composing three libraries rather than a new auth crate: figments-rs (figment2) profiles as the login switcher, jac-store-fjall (fjall backend) as the Jacquard SessionStore, and jacquard 0.12 for CredentialSession/Agent. Bootstrap wiring lives in a shared tassle-app crate consumed by both the CLI and a future web service. Amends ADR 0001: real auth lands as app-password (OAuth deferred). See doc/auth-design.md.

title: Gate corpus samples behind default feature
description: Add a samples feature to tass-lex-corpus, enabled by default, so embedded sample JSON and sample accessors are available normally but can be disabled by minimal consumers.
acceptance_criteria: tass-lex-corpus has default = [samples].; SampleRecord, SAMPLE_DIR, SAMPLES, iter_samples, and get_sample are gated behind the samples feature.; cargo test -p tass-lex-corpus passes with defaults.; cargo check -p tass-lex-corpus --no-default-features passes.; tassle-cli still builds with default corpus features.
notes: Added default-on samples feature to tass-lex-corpus. SampleRecord, SAMPLE_DIR, SAMPLES, iter_samples, get_sample, and sample tests are gated behind feature = samples. Lexicon JSON remains always available. Verified default tests, no-default-features check, and tassle-cli check.
close_reason: Default-on samples feature implemented and verified.

title: Move samples into corpus crate
description: Move generated sample records under crates/tass-lex-corpus so the corpus crate owns both lexicon JSON and canonical example record JSON. Expose samples from the corpus crate and update tassle samples to regenerate that directory by default.
acceptance_criteria: Sample JSON files live under crates/tass-lex-corpus/samples.; tass-lex-corpus embeds sample metadata/accessors.; tassle samples regenerates crates/tass-lex-corpus/samples by default.; README no longer references a root samples/ directory.; cargo test -p tass-lex-corpus and cargo check -p tassle-cli pass.
close_reason: Sample JSON moved into tass-lex-corpus and exposed by the corpus API; CLI default regeneration path updated and verified.

title: EventSource trait + normalized event envelope (spacedust / jetstream / atproto-tap)
description: Define an EventSource trait yielding a normalized envelope {source, did, collection, rkey, uri, cid, body?, kind, live, cursor} so listeners/fold are decoupled from the substrate. Hydrant is gone. Candidate impls: (1) Spacedust notification source (link pointers + hydration), (2) jetstream consumer (tass-at-large full bodies, cursor replay), (3) ngerakines atproto-tap. Spacedust yields pointers not bodies, so it carries a hydration adapter (Slingshot getRecordByUri, PDS fallback). Cursor-bearing sources (jetstream) support replay; Spacedust does not (see tass-backfill-constellation).
design: FINDING (hydrant assessment): hydrant DOES write full record bodies into fjall, not just metadata. RecordEvt.record is Arc<RawValue> (full record JSON); live:bool flags backfill(false) vs live(true); records persist to a 'blocks' keyspace and cursor-replay reads them back from disk; XRPC getRecordByUri/getUriRecord return {uri,cid,value}; countRecords exists. vs-tap doc: record events are replayable (global ordered event log + client cursor), identity/account are ephemeral; EPHEMERAL mode keeps a bounded TTL window. CONCLUSION: hydrant is a usable base-data event-source we can project off of WITHOUT fighting it or writing our own projections into it (user-keyspace exists if we ever want to). Candidate impls behind the trait: (1) embedded hydrant (tass-embed-hydrant) — own .data/hydrant/, narrow com.superbfowle.tass.* filter; (2) ngerakines atproto-tap (atproto-crates) — sharded /channel, server-managed cursors, no replay; (3) a jetstream consumer. Keep our own projections in our own LedgerStore regardless of source.

title: Rename Rust crates to tass prefixes
description: Rename current Rust workspace crates/packages from tassle-* to tass-* where they are crate/package prefixes. Target names: tass-cli, tass-lex, tass-codegen, tass-validate. Keep user-facing decisions separate: the binary command may remain tassle unless explicitly renamed later, but crate/package prefixes should not use tassle-* going forward. Update Cargo.toml package names, dependency names/imports, docs, and examples consistently.
acceptance_criteria: Rust workspace package names use tass-* prefixes.; Internal dependency names/import paths are updated.; README and codegen docs no longer instruct users to refer to tassle-* crate package names except historical notes if needed.; cargo check for the workspace passes.; The CLI binary naming decision is documented separately if it remains tassle.
notes: Extended scope to the data crate: renamed tass-lex-corpus -> tass-lex-schema (lexicons only, zero-dep) and split sample records into a new tass-lex-sample crate. "corpus" collided with the pub.layers.corpus domain concept and mislabeled schema-as-data; "schema" names the lexicon source-of-truth accurately. tass-lex remains reserved for the renamed tassle-lexicons (generated). Remaining tassle-* -> tass-* renames (cli, codegen, validate, ledger) still pending. Also added xtask/ (codegen + samples) per the samples-generator decision.

title: Add pure lexicon corpus package
description:
Create a source-of-truth package/crate for lexicon documents themselves, separate from generated Rust bindings. This should expose our authored lexicon JSON in a semi-pure form with minimal or no Rust logic: canonical JSON files, metadata/manifest, reproducible discovery, and optional tiny helpers to iterate embedded documents. Generated crates such as tass-lex and future tass-lex-mage should consume this corpus rather than treating generated Rust as the source.

The goal is to keep lexicons as data first. Rust bindings, validators, docs, and sample generators are downstream artifacts. Use tass-* names everywhere; do not introduce new tassle-* package/crate prefixes.
acceptance_criteria: Canonical com.superbfowle.tass.* lexicon JSON has a dedicated package/corpus boundary.; Generated Rust bindings are clearly downstream of the corpus.; Corpus can be consumed by codegen/tests without copying paths ad hoc.; The package contains minimal Rust, preferably only include_str/include_dir style accessors if it is a crate at all.; README/docs explain source-of-truth versus generated artifacts.; New package/crate names use tass-* prefixes, not tassle-* prefixes.
notes: Implemented initial data-first corpus crate at crates/tass-lex-corpus. It embeds the existing com.superbfowle.tass.* JSON documents via include_str!, exposes deterministic iter()/get() accessors, and has a smoke test covering the six current lexicons. The JSON files remain the source input for now; generated Rust bindings stay downstream.
close_reason: Initial tass-lex-corpus crate added and verified.

title: Clean up tass lexicon codegen
description:
Improve the existing generated Rust binding path for our authored com.superbfowle.tass.* lexicons. Current generated files compile but emit repeated unused import warnings, and validation registration is manual in tassle-validate because generated LexiconSchema impls are not inventory-submitted. This ticket is for our own tass lexicons, separate from third-party rpg.actor generation and Mage-focused modeling.

Naming direction: generated bindings should become tass-lex, with tass-lex-corpus as the source data package. Do not add new tassle-* crate names.
acceptance_criteria: Generated tass-lex bindings compile without repeated unused-import warnings or warnings are intentionally suppressed in generated code.; Validation no longer needs manual inventory registration for every generated schema, or the limitation is documented with a codegen follow-up.; Regeneration command remains documented and reproducible.; No rpg.actor scope is added to this crate by accident.; Names use tass-* prefixes, not tassle-* prefixes.
notes: New prerequisite/design direction: introduce tass-lex-corpus as the data-first source of lexicon JSON. tass-lex should be treated as generated Rust bindings downstream of that corpus, not as the canonical lexicon store.

title: Patch rpg.actor stats lexicon for Jacquard codegen
description:
Make the third-party actor.rpg.stats lexicon usable with Jacquard codegen without forking semantics unnecessarily. Current blockers found by test generation: 12 object schemas without properties in Pathfinder/Starfinder free-form maps, and one string format date at spotemStats.lastDaily. A mechanical preprocessing layer adding properties: {} to free-form object schemas and dropping unsupported format: date allowed full codegen to complete in .test-agent.

This is about full third-party lexicon generation. It is separate from tassle-lex-mage, which can be narrower and should focus on the current Mage payload.
acceptance_criteria: Document the exact rpg.actor stats codegen incompatibilities.; Decide whether to patch upstream copy, preprocess before codegen, or improve Jacquard parser tolerance.; Full actor.rpg.stats codegen succeeds in a reproducible command.; Generated output compiles or compile blockers are recorded.; Do not block the narrow tassle-lex-mage model on full rpg.actor generation.

title: Add tass-lex-mage model for actor.rpg.stats/mage
description:
Create a narrow Mage-focused Rust model for current actor.rpg.stats/mage records. This should use the current per-system rkey envelope and lowercase mageStats payload from rpg.actor. Do not spend effort preserving old capitalized legacy self.mage data; legacy data is not relevant going forward.

The model should cover mageStats, mageVariableStat, and mageWillpower first, then feed mage stats output and future ledger validation context. It may be handwritten initially or generated from a curated/patched Mage-only lexicon subset, but it should not pretend to be the full actor.rpg.stats implementation. The package/crate name should be tass-lex-mage, not tassle-lex-mage.
acceptance_criteria: Typed MageStats model exists with serde support.; actor.rpg.stats/mage per-system envelope can deserialize into the model.; mage stats command uses the model instead of ad-hoc serde_json field reads.; Legacy capitalized self.mage compatibility is not required for the new model.; Willpower permanent/temporary, backgrounds, quintessence, paradox, spheres, and meditation are represented.

title: Ledger fold over the EventSource stream (normalize -> per-DID balances + snapshot/cursor)
description: Fold normalized EventSource events (jetstream tass-at-large source) into per-DID derived ledger state in fjall, with snapshot + cursor commit semantics (advance cursor only after durable commit). Consumes the tass-source-trait envelope, not Hydrant. Tracks every enervate/tassilize/meditate network-wide so each tass's real remaining quintessence is known independent of any command.
design: Tallying: store the normalized event log per DID in fjall (durable, ordered) AND a materialized balance snapshot + last-folded cursor. Rebuild snapshot by replay when missing/stale — same as statusphere's 'replay from 0 to rebuild index', but persisted. Gives recomputability (ledger.md's core property) + O(1) reads. Time-based Node regen / half-life ticks (see tass-half-life-ticks) settle lazily on read (lazy-mint-on-touch keeps energy bounded, per design doc open question). Read cmds first: tassle ledger balance|history|inspect <uri>. Reuse the CEL item envelope from ledger.md so filtering matches other list/read cmds. Validation hook (meditate/tassilize/enervate --dry-run, force flag) comes after the fold reads clean.
acceptance_criteria: tassle ledger balance/history/inspect read from folded fjall state; snapshot recomputable from event log replay; anomalies displayed not hidden.

title: Per-DID fjall ledger store (LedgerStore trait, lazy-open keyspace LRU)
description: Derived ledger/service state needs durable storage in fjall. User wants one fjall db (keyspace) per DID: per-DID WAL + locking, independent lifecycle (rm dir to forget a DID), independent backup/snapshot, small corruption blast radius — mirrors atproto's per-repo sovereignty model. Cost: fjall is designed for ONE keyspace with MANY partitions, not many keyspaces; each keyspace has its own journal, memtables, flush/compaction threads, and FDs. Many DIDs = many small files + many FDs.
design: Recommend a LedgerStore trait so the fold doesn't care about physical layout. Two layouts behind it: (A) one keyspace, one partition per DID (or partition-per-collection with DID key prefix) — fewest files, shared journal/fsync, coarser isolation; (B) per-DID keyspace directory — fine-grained locking + snapshot story the user wants. To bound the per-DID-keyspace cost: lazy-open on touch + LRU of open keyspaces (close idle ones) so you never hold 10k keyspaces open at once. Use the SAME fjall version as hydrant (via hydrant's user-keyspace feature reexport, or pin 90-008/fjall rev) to avoid format/symbol conflicts. Data dir layout: .data/ledger/<did>/ per DID -> feeds the btrfs subvolume ticket.
